# Jinja2 Templates Instructions

## Overview

Jinja2 templates are used to dynamically generate configuration files with variable substitution. Primarily used for Docker Compose files and application configuration files in Ansible roles.

## Project Structure

```
iac/ansible/roles/
├── apps/<appname>/templates/
│   ├── docker-compose.yml.j2      # Docker Compose definition
│   ├── <appname>-config.j2        # App-specific config
│   └── <appname>-settings.yaml.j2 # Settings template
└── base/*/templates/
    ├── sshd_config.j2             # SSH daemon configuration
    └── other-config.j2            # Other system configs
```

## File Naming Convention

### Template Files

- `.j2` extension for all Jinja2 files
- Meaningful names matching output file
- Examples:
  - `docker-compose.yml.j2` → renders to `docker-compose.yml`
  - `settings.yaml.j2` → renders to `settings.yaml`
  - `sshd_config.j2` → renders to `sshd_config`

## Template Syntax

### Variable Interpolation

```jinja2
# Simple variable
{{ variable_name }}

# Nested variables
{{ parent.child.value }}

# Filters
{{ variable | upper }}
{{ variable | default('fallback_value') }}

# Expressions
{{ port + 1 }}
{{ enabled | bool }}
```

### Control Structures

#### Conditionals

```jinja2
{% if enable_feature %}
  # Configuration when enabled
{% elif alternative_condition %}
  # Alternative configuration
{% else %}
  # Default configuration
{% endif %}
```

#### Loops

```jinja2
{% for item in list_variable %}
  - name: {{ item.name }}
    port: {{ item.port }}
{% endfor %}

# Loop with conditionals
{% for item in items %}
  {% if item.enabled %}
    - name: {{ item.name }}
  {% endif %}
{% endfor %}
```

### Comments

```jinja2
{# This comment will not appear in rendered output #}
# Regular comment that will appear in output

{# Multi-line comment
   Can span multiple lines
   Will not appear in output #}
```

## Docker Compose Template Pattern

Typical Docker Compose template structure:

```jinja2
version: '3.8'

services:
  appname:
    image: "{{ docker_image }}:{{ image_tag }}"
    container_name: "{{ container_name }}"
    restart: {{ restart_policy | default('unless-stopped') }}

    environment:
      - TZ={{ timezone }}
      - LOG_LEVEL={{ log_level | upper }}

    {% if enable_volumes %}
    volumes:
      - "{{ volume_host_path }}:{{ volume_container_path }}"
    {% endif %}

    ports:
      {% for port in container_ports %}
      - "{{ port.host }}:{{ port.container }}"
      {% endfor %}

    {% if traefik_enabled %}
    labels:
      - traefik.enable=true
      - traefik.http.routers.{{ service_name }}.rule=Host(`{{ service_domain }}`)
    {% endif %}

    networks:
      - traefik

networks:
  traefik:
    external: true
```

## Configuration File Template Pattern

For system configuration files:

```jinja2
# {{ ansible_managed }}
# This file is auto-generated by Ansible
# Changes will be overwritten on next playbook run

[section]
# Basic configuration
parameter1 = {{ param1_value }}
parameter2 = {{ param2_value | quote }}

# Conditional sections
{% if advanced_config %}
[advanced]
debug_mode = {{ debug | lower }}
timeout = {{ timeout_seconds }}
{% endif %}

# Loop-generated section
{% for server in upstream_servers %}
upstream_server_{{ loop.index }} = {{ server.host }}:{{ server.port }}
{% endfor %}
```

## Application Settings Pattern

For YAML configuration files:

```jinja2
---
# Generated by Ansible - do not edit manually

app:
  name: {{ app_name }}
  version: {{ app_version }}
  debug: {{ enable_debug | lower }}

database:
  host: {{ db_host }}
  port: {{ db_port }}
  name: {{ db_name }}
  {% if db_credentials %}
  username: {{ db_username }}
  password: {{ db_password }}
  {% endif %}

services:
  {% for service in enabled_services %}
  - name: {{ service.name }}
    enabled: true
    configuration:
      {% for key, value in service.config.items() %}
      {{ key }}: {{ value }}
      {% endfor %}
  {% endfor %}
```

## Built-in Filters

### String Filters

```jinja2
{{ variable | upper }}           # Convert to uppercase
{{ variable | lower }}           # Convert to lowercase
{{ variable | capitalize }}      # Capitalize first letter
{{ variable | replace('old', 'new') }}  # Replace substring
{{ variable | length }}          # Get length
{{ variable | quote }}           # Add quotes (for CLI arguments)
```

### List/Dict Filters

```jinja2
{{ list | first }}               # First element
{{ list | last }}                # Last element
{{ list | length }}              # List length
{{ dict | list }}                # Convert dict keys to list
{{ varname | default('fallback') }}  # Default if undefined
```

### Type Filters

```jinja2
{{ variable | bool }}            # Convert to boolean
{{ variable | int }}             # Convert to integer
{{ variable | string }}          # Convert to string
```

### Test Filters

```jinja2
{% if variable | defined %}      # Check if defined
{% if variable is defined %}     # Alternative syntax
{% if variable is not defined %}
{% if variable | changed %}      # Check if changed
```

## Ansible Integration

### Template Module

```yaml
- name: Deploy application configuration
  ansible.builtin.template:
    src: application.conf.j2 # Template path (relative to templates/)
    dest: /etc/application/app.conf # Destination on target
    owner: root # File owner
    group: root # File group
    mode: "0644" # File permissions
    backup: yes # Backup before overwrite
```

### Available Ansible Variables

Templates have access to all Ansible variables:

- Play variables
- Role variables (from defaults/, vars/)
- Inventory variables
- Host facts
- Registered variables

### Variable Precedence (Highest to Lowest)

1. Task variables
2. Role variables (`roles/role/vars/`)
3. Role defaults (`roles/role/defaults/`)
4. Inventory group variables
5. Inventory host variables
6. Host facts

## Best Practices

### Template Organization

- Keep templates logically organized by application/role
- Use descriptive names matching output files
- Include comments explaining complex logic
- Document required variables in role README

### Readability

- Use comments to explain template logic
- Format conditionals for clarity
- Use consistent indentation
- Keep line lengths reasonable (80 columns)

### Variable Naming in Templates

- Use clear, descriptive variable names
- Follow role variable naming conventions
- Prefix with role name when possible
- Document all expected variables

### Safety and Security

- Always use `| quote` for CLI arguments
- Properly escape sensitive values
- Keep templates version-controlled
- Document sensitive variables required

### Conditional Inclusion

- Use conditionals for optional features
- Provide sensible defaults with `| default()`
- Test both true and false conditions
- Validate template with `ansible-playbook --check`

## Common Patterns

### Optional Configuration Block

```jinja2
{% if optional_feature | default(false) %}
# Configuration block only included if enabled
feature_enabled = true
{% for option in feature_options %}
option_{{ loop.index }} = {{ option }}
{% endfor %}
{% endif %}
```

### For Multi-Line Values

```jinja2
description: |
  {{ app_description }}

  This is a multi-line value
  that preserves formatting
```

### Conditional Defaults

```jinja2
timeout: {{ timeout_value | default(30 if environment == 'production' else 60) }}
```

### Environment-Specific Values

```jinja2
log_level: "{{ log_level | default('INFO' if environment == 'production' else 'DEBUG') }}"
```

## Testing Templates

### Validate Template Syntax

Use Ansible's template testing:

```bash
ansible-playbook playbook.yml --check
```

### With Verbosity

```bash
ansible-playbook playbook.yml --check -vvv
```

### Debug Template Rendering

Add to playbook:

```yaml
- name: Debug template variables
  debug:
    var: variable_name
```

## Troubleshooting

### Variable Undefined Error

```
UndefinedError: 'variable_name' is undefined
```

- Check variable spelling (case-sensitive)
- Verify variable defined in defaults/ or inventory
- Check variable scope (role, play, block, task)
- Use `| default()` filter for optional variables

### Syntax Error in Template

```
TemplateAssertionError: ... has a parse error
```

- Check Jinja2 syntax validity
- Verify proper whitespace handling
- Check filter syntax
- Validate brackets/braces match

### File Not Found

```
TemplateNotFound: template.j2
```

- Verify template in `templates/` directory
- Check file extension is `.j2`
- Verify path relative to role directory
- Check file permissions
